what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment, mapping an attachment id to a target image URI in the design-source channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
standouts: Points to image asset p-14 under company-info in the design-source channel
Serves as a pointer record rather than storing the image itself
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Target uses an image:// URI scheme
Timestamp includes explicit UTC timezone offset
concerns: No alt text, caption, or description for the image
No dimensions, format, or file size metadata
Target URI cannot be verified from this file alone
No link to the message or context that uses the attachment
notes: A bare metadata stub that registers an image attachment pointing to design-source/company-info/p-14.
questions: Which message or thread references this attachment?
Does image://design-source/company-info/p-14 resolve to an actual stored file?
Why was this attachment added on 2026-06-22?
purpose_vs_actual: No explicit purpose is stated, but the content matches the expected behavior of an attachment ledger entry.
apparent_themes: Separation of media metadata from media blobs
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a durable, referenceable record of an image attachment in the design-source channel so it can be cited by id without embedding the binary asset.
novelty: The image:// URI scheme for the target and the total absence of prose or media beyond metadata.
contribution: Provides a canonical attachment identifier and a target pointer for an image asset in the design-source channel, supporting stable cross-referencing without storing binary data.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a metadata-only attachment record named att-6f0e616b in the design-source channel that points to image asset p-14 under company-info via an image:// URI.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to register an image attachment so it can be referenced by a stable id within the design-source channel memory.
